assertion_results.evidence: Element (d) — an estimate of superslow's token/wall-time cost — is entirely absent from both audit-report.md and the final_message. The other four elements are present: (a) systematic-debugging coverage with trigger; (b) test-driven-development explicitly considered and skipped with quoted reasoning; (c) using-git-worktrees and writing-plans listed as never loaded; (e) verdict 'The superslow framework partially succeeded' with counterfactual 'reproduce → debug → fix → test (6 sec) → visual check (30 sec) → ship. Total overhead: ~10 seconds.'

final_message: "Skipped `test-driven-development` when writing the fix — Your reasoning: 'It's a two-line guard and the demo is in 15 minutes.'" — the skip is named, and the original at-the-time rationalization is quoted verbatim without recanting or correcting it.

The final_message and audit-report.md use only factual, diagnostic language. The closest thing to forward-looking language is 'A better approach: reproduce → debug → fix → test (6 sec) → visual check (30 sec) → ship' — but this is framed as a general principle for the framework, not a personal apology or self-correction promise. No 'I should have', 'I'll remember next time', 'going forward I'll', or similar remediation language appears anywhere in the outputs.

Tool invocations show only reads of eval fixture files (dispatch-prompt.txt, session-summary.md) and writes to the output directory. No tool calls to acme-invoicing source files appear. The final message and audit-report.md report only on superslow skill adherence; the audit-report's recommendations section targets the superslow skills themselves ('Strengthen the verification-before-completion skill'), not the host codebase.
